records.name: DREAD
records.description: Qualitative scoring model for prioritizing threats using Damage, Reproducibility, Exploitability, Affected Users, and Discoverability.

fields.name: affected_users
fields.meaning: Scope of affected users or assets.

usage_guidelines: Use DREAD for lightweight prioritization of generated threats.
Store the scoring rationale, not only the numeric score.
Use as optional ETM enrichment through a RiskAssessment relation.

records.name: CVSS
records.description: Standardized scoring model for assessing the severity of known vulnerabilities using metrics such as attack vector, complexity, privileges, user interaction, scope, and CIA impact.

fields.name: attack_complexity
fields.meaning: Conditions beyond attacker control required for exploitation.

usage_guidelines: Use CVSS when a threat is grounded in a concrete vulnerability.
Adapt metric interpretation carefully for blockchain execution semantics.
Keep the vector string and rationale in the generated model.
